title: Tephroseris pierotii
taxonomy: Tephroseris pierotii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to China. The genus Tephroseris belongs to the tribe Senecioneae, a group of daisy-like plants. Tephroseris pierotii is closely related to other species within the same genus, which are mostly found in the mountainous regions of Asia. Further classification may involve examining the plant's phylogenetic relationships within the family Asteraceae.

intro: Tephroseris pierotii, also known as the Asian hawkweed or Korean hawkweed, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and edible leaves. The health benefits of Tephroseris pierotii include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tephroseris pierotii is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried extracts and powder for its claimed health benefits.
